claimText: Prithvi is a short-range surface-to-surface ballistic missile developed under the Integrated Guided Missile Development Program (IGMDP).
examUse: Correct-pair questions matching missile names to their operational types.

claimText: Under the IGMDP, Trishul is a short-range surface-to-air missile, whereas Akash is a medium-range surface-to-air missile.
evidenceExcerpt: Trishul: Short-range low-level surface-to-air missile. Akash: Medium-range surface-to-air missile.
examUse: Statement-based comparison questions testing the range and trajectory types of IGMDP missiles.

claimText: Nag is a third-generation anti-tank missile developed as part of the IGMDP.
examUse: Correct-pair questions identifying the specific role of the Nag missile.

claimText: Agni Prime is a two-stage, solid-fueled, canister-launched ballistic missile capable of carrying both conventional and nuclear warheads.
evidenceExcerpt: Two-stage, solid-fueled, canister-launched ballistic missile. It can carry both conventional and nuclear warheads.
examUse: Statement-based questions on the technical specifications and payload capacity of the Agni Prime missile.
distractorHooks: Capable of carrying only conventional warheads

claimText: Agni Prime is the first of the new generation of missiles developed after the end of the IGMDP and utilizes a rail-based mobile launcher system.
evidenceExcerpt: Agni Prime: DRDO & Strategic Force Command (SFC) successfully launched the Agni-Prime Missile from a Rail based Mobile launcher system... First of the new generation of missiles after the end of the IGMDP.
examUse: Statement-based questions regarding the development timeline and launch mechanism of Agni Prime.
